sections.paragraph:
διαδίδωμι fut. διαδώσω LXX; 1 aor. διέδωκα, 2 aor. impv. διάδος. Pass.: impf. 3 sg. διεδίδετο; aor. 3 sg. διεδόθη (PsSol 1:4); ptc. διαδοθείς LXX; (s. on this form B-D-F §94, 1; W-S. §14, 11; Mlt-H. 206) to apportion among various parties, distribute, give (so Thu. et al.; X., Cyr. 1, 3, 7; SIG 374, 12; POxy 1115, 6; 1194, 17; PLips 35, 10; LXX; TestJob; TestBenj 11:1) τὰ σκῦλα the spoils Lk 11:22. τί τινι (Thu. 4, 38, 4; Jos., Ant. 7, 86) 18:22; Rv 17:13 t.r. (Erasmian rdg. s. Tdf. app.; RBorger, TRu 52, ’87, 57); τὰ λοιπὰ τοῖς συνδούλοις the rest to his fellow slaves Hs 5, 2, 9. In Lk 12:42 v.l. the recipient of τὸ σιτομέτριον is implied. τινί to someone J 6:11 (v.l. ἔδωκεν N25 app., Tdf. in text; s. RBorger, op. cit, 34f). Pass. Ac 4:35.—M-M.

fut. fut. = fut.

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

aor. aor. = aorist

impv. impv. = imperative

impf. impf. = imperfect

  1. sg. = singular

PsSol PsSol = Psalms of Solomon—List 2

ptc. ptc. = participle

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

W-S. W-S. = GWiner, Grammatik des neutestamentlichen Sprachidioms, ed. PSchmiedel—List 6

Mlt-H. Mlt-H. = JMoulton/WHoward, A Grammar of NT Greek II: Accidence and Word-formation—List 6

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

PLips PLips = Griech. Urkunden der Papyrussammlung zu Leipzig—List 4

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

TestBenj TestBenj = Testament of Benjamin, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

t.r. t.r. = textus receptus

rdg. rdg. = reading(s)

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

app. app. = appendix, apparatus (when ref. to marginal text-critical information, esp. in N.)

TRu TRu = Theologische Rundschau—List 6

Hs Hs = Similitudes

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 227
